page: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 2388G processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. This CPU features 8 physical cores and 16 virtual cores operating at a base frequency of 3.2 GHz, supported by a 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage consists of a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present on this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% and single-threaded gzip compression reaching top-tier status in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores, falls within the average range. Memory performance is mixed; while the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 10%, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks are relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform at average levels, processing 16.84 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers a high density of local NVMe storage and memory relative to its 8-core CPU profile, making it a viable option for workloads requiring dedicated hardware without the overhead of virtualization. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage, strong single-threaded performance, and 128.0 GB of RAM makes this server suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and memory-intensive applications such as caching layers or data processing pipelines that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is a bare metal server configured with an Intel Xeon 2388G processor on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 8 physical cores, 16 vCPUs, and a 3.2 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, offering a high-capacity storage and memory profile without a dedicated GPU. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded compression, though large-block uncached memory bandwidth is relatively weak. Multi-core CPU metrics and database operations perform at average levels. Given its dedicated hardware resources, high memory capacity, and fast local NVMe storage, this server is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose workloads requiring dedicated, non-virtualized compute resources.
og_description: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is an x86_64 bare metal server featuring an 8-core Intel Xeon 2388G CPU, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for database hosting and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-8c-132gb-v2 is a bare metal server with an Intel Xeon CPU, 128 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe storage, designed for database and memory workloads.
tagline: A bare metal server combining strong single-core performance, high-capacity NVMe storage, and dense memory for dedicated database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
